message:
Fix negative numkeys validation in BLMPOP/BZMPOP and migrate replication tests to conformance fixtures

BLMPOP/BZMPOP (crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs):
- Negative numkeys values were silently cast to usize via `as usize`,
  wrapping to a huge value and causing either a panic on bounds check or
  an incorrect syntax-error path. Now reject numkeys <= 0 early with
  SyntaxError before the cast, matching Redis server behavior.
- Split the combined `numkeys == 0 || argv.len() < ...` guard into
  separate checks so the negative/zero case gets the correct error type
  (SyntaxError) rather than falling through to a generic syntax-error
  string response.

Replication conformance fixtures (new file):
- Add crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/core_replication.json covering
  REPLCONF (ACK, listening-port, capa variants, ip-address, GETACK,
  wrong arity), PSYNC (initial fullresync, with replid, wrong arity),
  REPLICAOF/SLAVEOF (NO ONE, with host:port, wrong arity), and ROLE
  (master default response).
- Remove the corresponding inline #[test] functions (replicaof_no_one,
  slaveof_no_one, role_returns_master) from lib.rs since they are now
  covered by the fixture-driven conformance runner with richer assertion
  capabilities.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
